message:
fix: use valid adjective+noun agent name in E2E test

Changed AGENT_NAME from 'Me' to 'BlueLake' in the E2E integration test.

Agent names MUST follow the adjective+noun pseudonym format (BlueMountain,
GreenLake, RedSunset, etc.) - this is a core design principle of the system
where agent names are deliberately meaningless random combinations.

'Me' is not a valid agent name format and violated the naming convention.
'BlueLake' is a proper adjective+noun combination that follows the rules.

Test continues to pass and properly validates file reservation conflict
detection with a correctly formatted agent name.

files.patch:
@@ -160,7 +160,7 @@ async def _call_tool(tool_name: str, args: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
     nul_payload = "src/shared.txt\0".encode("utf-8")
     check_cmd = [sys.executable, "-m", "mcp_agent_mail.cli", "guard", "check", "--stdin-nul", "--repo", str(repo)]
     env_with_agent = os.environ.copy()
-    env_with_agent["AGENT_NAME"] = "Me"
+    env_with_agent["AGENT_NAME"] = "BlueLake"  # Valid adjective+noun format
     rc = subprocess.run(check_cmd, input=nul_payload, env=env_with_agent, cwd=str(wt)).returncode
     console.print(Panel.fit(f"guard check rc={rc}", title="guard check result"))
     assert rc == 1  # conflict expected
